title: [Checkpointing P0] Orchestrate checkpoint restore and boundary-aware recovery in MoonMind.UserWorkflow
acceptance_criteria: `MoonMind.UserWorkflow` has an explicit checkpoint-recovery state machine rather than a metadata-only restore marker.
Recovery preflight validates action, phase, boundary, checkpoint kind, capability snapshot, restore route, and side-effect safety before scheduling work.
The restore route is resolved from the frozen capability snapshot through the activity catalog.
The destination agent-run identity and restore idempotency key are deterministic and recorded before restore scheduling.
No recovered agent launches until a verified destination locator and restoration evidence are present.
The source locator and source absolute path are never used as the destination workspace.
`before_execution` recovery restores and runs the failed step exactly once as a new Step Execution.
`after_execution` recovery resumes at the gate/candidate phase without rerunning the completed mutating execution.
`after_gate` recovery continues after an accepted gate without rerunning implementation or verification.
`before_publication` recovery resumes publication without rerunning implementation or verification.
`before_recovery_restoration` retries restoration without creating a business Step Execution.
Unknown or contradictory boundary/phase combinations fail closed.
Earlier accepted steps are materialized as preserved provenance and are not rerun.
New semantic work receives correct local identity and cross-run lineage.
Restoration retries do not increment business Step Execution ordinals.
Side-effect dispositions remain authoritative after restore.
Restoration outcome is independently visible in ledger, run summary, and incident evidence.
Workflow/worker restarts and duplicate requests reconcile idempotently.
Existing Temporal histories replay across explicit patch/version cutovers.
